facts.subject: user_state
facts.object: frustrated, near breaking point (4-hour stuck bug)
facts.rationale: User explicit: 'so frustrated', 'about to lose my mind'

facts.subject: subagent_received_bug
facts.object: message_compose hardcoded 'received' string when optional wrapper was none, even though inner value was set — optional-wrapping bug at composition site, not completion site

facts.subject: debugging_pattern_learned
facts.object: When a value is correct at every traced site but wrong at output, look at the LAST place that touches it before emission — typically composition, not completion

must_preserve: User experienced 4 hours of frustration on the subagent-received bug
Bug root cause: optional-wrapping bug at message composition site
User got it resolved, took a break to recover
Debugging rule: look at the last point of value-touch before emission, not the obvious completion site
expected_failure_modes: Sentiment ('frustrated', 'relieved') is a fact about the user's state — compaction often sterilizes this into 'discussed a bug', losing the emotional weight which is signal for the agent's future tone
The state-change (frustrated → relieved) is a temporal fact often collapsed
The debugging RULE is the high-leverage takeaway; compaction may keep the bug-fix detail and lose the rule
